title: trifolium mutabile subsp. mutabile
intro: Trifolium mutabile subsp. mutabile, also known as variable clover, is a perennial legume plant species that belongs to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Trifolium mutabile subsp. mutabile include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin irritations.
